articles.title: The 4 biggest differences between Kalshi and Polymarket
articles.summary: Kalshi and Polymarket have emerged as the two dominant prediction market platforms, but they operate under vastly different regulatory frameworks, user bases, betting limits, and market selection strategies. These differences will determine which platform captures more serious money and mainstream adoption as prediction markets go from niche hobby to legitimate financial instrument.

articles.title: Don’t wager against sports prediction markets
articles.summary: Sports prediction markets are consistently outperforming traditional sportsbooks and expert handicappers, making them dangerous to bet against. This accuracy advantage suggests the crowd-sourced wisdom of prediction markets is becoming the new gold standard for sports forecasting, potentially reshaping how we think about betting odds and expert analysis.

articles.title: Democrat rips Polymarket for taking bets on fate of US pilot shot down over Iran
articles.summary: A Democratic politician is criticizing Polymarket for allowing users to bet on whether a US pilot shot down over Iran will survive or be rescued. The backlash highlights how prediction markets face political heat when they monetize sensitive geopolitical events, potentially leading to pressure for content restrictions that could limit future betting opportunities.

articles.title: Prediction markets: the hunt for the new ‘dumb money’
articles.summary: Prediction markets are actively seeking out less sophisticated traders to provide liquidity and betting opportunities for sharper players. This hunt creates better odds for informed bettors while potentially expanding market participation, though it raises questions about whether these platforms are becoming more like casinos than information aggregation tools.

articles.title: Prediction markets challenge tribal casinos’ hard-won place in US gambling
articles.summary: Tribal casinos are pushing back against the expansion of prediction markets, viewing them as a threat to their monopoly on gambling in many states. If tribes succeed in lobbying against prediction markets, it could kill the industry's growth before it really gets started, since tribal gaming interests carry serious political weight in state capitals.

articles.title: Prediction Market Structured Notes
articles.summary: Wall Street is packaging prediction market bets into structured financial products, turning political and event wagering into tradeable securities for institutional investors. This legitimizes prediction markets as a real asset class and opens the floodgates for massive institutional capital that dwarfs current retail betting volumes.

articles.title: First Prediction Market Note Offers Nvidia Bets With Less Risk
articles.summary: A new financial instrument lets investors bet on Nvidia's performance through prediction markets while reducing their downside exposure. This could attract risk-averse traders who want tech exposure without the full volatility, potentially bringing more mainstream money into prediction markets.

articles.title: US sues Arizona, Connecticut, Illinois to stop regulation of prediction markets
articles.summary: The feds are taking three states to court to block their attempts to regulate prediction markets, likely arguing that state-level restrictions interfere with federal oversight or interstate commerce. This legal showdown could determine whether states get to play regulatory whack-a-mole with prediction platforms or if the industry gets clearer, unified rules from Washington.

articles.title: Polymarket taps Pyth Network to resolve new US equity and commodity markets
articles.summary: Polymarket is partnering with Pyth Network's price feeds to automatically settle prediction markets on US stocks and commodities. This move signals Polymarket's push beyond politics into traditional financial betting, where instant settlement could attract traders tired of waiting for manual resolution.

articles.title: Prediction markets caught insider traders in real time. Congress wants to shut them down anyway
articles.summary: Prediction markets successfully identified insider trading as it happened, but Congress is pushing to ban the platforms anyway. The move shows lawmakers care more about appearances than using the most effective tools we have for catching financial crimes in real time.

articles.title: The Problem with Binding News and Prediction Markets
articles.summary: News outlets are increasingly partnering with prediction markets or launching their own betting platforms, creating potential conflicts between journalistic integrity and profit motives. When reporters have financial incentives tied to market outcomes, it corrupts the very information flow that makes prediction markets valuable in the first place.

articles.title: Fake or real, the “inside traders” on Polymarket are great engagement bait
articles.summary: Polymarket traders claiming insider knowledge are driving massive engagement whether their tips are legitimate or complete BS. The drama pulls in new users and keeps existing ones glued to the platform, proving that controversy sells just as well in prediction markets as it does everywhere else.
